codeReview.summary: Pass. The prior blocking issue is fixed: `prepare_model_input_items` now preserves caller-supplied items and only appends generated run items (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/items.rs:32-40`), while hosted-tool sanitation remains scoped to `compose_replay_input_items` and replay/resume callers such as `RunState::resume_input` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/items.rs:45-52`, `crates/agents-core/src/run_state.rs:310-315`). Existing parity coverage still validates websocket URL/payload normalization and replay orphan pruning (`crates/agents-openai/src/websocket.rs:37-110`, `crates/agents-openai/tests/hosted_tool_and_websocket_parity.rs:6-112`), and the added facade test confirms all five hosted helper constructors remain importable from `openai_agents` (`crates/openai-agents/src/lib.rs:88-102`, `crates/openai-agents/tests/hosted_tool_helpers_semantics.rs:1-26`).

summary: Reviewed the latest handoff, transcript skeleton, commit 3f19b8052739e0ffccbcb8c294feb503a0d4c687, and the current hosted-tool/websocket parity surface. The replay sanitizer is now confined to replay/reconstruction paths, ordinary caller-provided hosted-tool items remain intact during fresh model-input preparation, and I found no remaining blocking or non-blocking issues in the changed surface.
